tools.namespace_description: Use Sites to build, save, deploy, and inspect websites such as landing pages, portfolios, dashboards, portals, trackers, hubs, games, and internal tools. Always use Sites when .openai/hosting.json exists. Use Sites skills for local implementation, validation, source preparation, and artifact packaging. Use this connector for site creation, runtime environment variables, versions, production deployments, and access controls. Read .openai/hosting.json before creating a site and reuse its project_id when present. Treat Sites IDs and cursors as opaque: copy them exactly from .openai/hosting.json or Sites responses as applicable, and never invent, reformat, derive, or substitute them. Never call create_site more than once for the same local site. Push the exact source state before saving a version. commit_sha must identify that pushed state, and any archive must be built from it. Deploy only saved versions; every Sites deployment URL is production. Inspect deployment status when the initial result is non-terminal or the user asks for progress. Unless the user asks for local-only work or a saved version without deployment, finish deployable site work with a production deployment.

tool.name: sites.add_custom_domain
tool.title: add_custom_domain
tool.description: Add a custom domain to a published site. The response includes a CNAME target for subdomains, A record targets for zone apex domains, and all App Garden and Cloudflare validation records that must be set before the custom domain can route to the Site.

project_id.description: Exact opaque site project ID. Copy it verbatim from .openai/hosting.json's project_id or the id field returned by create_site, list_sites, or get_site. Never invent, modify, or substitute another identifier.
project_id.title: Project Id

hostname.description: Bare custom hostname, such as www.example.com
hostname.title: Hostname

cname_target.description: CNAME target to use for custom subdomains.
cname_target.title: Cname Target

apex_proxy_ipv4_targets.description: A record targets to use when the custom hostname is a zone apex.
apex_proxy_ipv4_targets.title: Apex Proxy Ipv4 Targets

tool.name: sites.create_site
tool.title: create_site
tool.description: Create a site only when .openai/hosting.json has no project_id. If it has one, reuse that site. Never call this tool more than once for the same local site. This tool does not create local source. Immediately persist the response's id unchanged as project_id in .openai/hosting.json. The response includes a short-lived source repository credential that can be reused for pushes until it expires. Use per-command Git authentication; never expose or persist its token.

slug.description: Unique URL slug for the site. Use at least 5 characters, starting with a lowercase ASCII letter and containing only lowercase ASCII letters, digits, and single hyphens. Do not use leading, trailing, or consecutive hyphens, a reserved Sites slug, or a slug already used by another site.
slug.title: Slug

description.description: Optional user-facing description of the site.
description.title: Description

id.description: Opaque site project ID. Pass this exact value as project_id.
id.title: Id

repository.description: Provider repository name bound to the AppGen project.
repository.title: Repository

branch.description: Default branch the client should push.
branch.title: Branch

remote_url.description: Git remote URL without embedded credentials.
remote_url.title: Remote Url

auth_mode.description: Git authentication mode for the token.
auth_mode.title: Auth Mode

source_repository_credential.description: Short-lived source repository write credential when requested.

tool.name: sites.create_source_repository_write_credential
tool.title: create_source_repository_write_credential
tool.description: Create a short-lived source repository write credential when the credential returned by create_site is no longer usable. Use it to push the source state later referenced by commit_sha. The credential can be reused until it expires; use per-command Git authentication. Never expose or persist its token.

project_id.title: Project Id

repository.title: Repository

branch.title: Branch

remote_url.description: Git remote URL without embedded credentials.
remote_url.title: Remote Url

tool.name: sites.deploy_private_site_version
tool.title: deploy_private_site_version
tool.description: Deploy a saved site version to production only when verified owner-only access makes the current caller the sole explicitly allowed viewer and allows no groups. This tool fails without starting a deployment when the site is shared, public, or cannot be verified as owner-only. In those cases, ask the user to approve deployment before using deploy_site_version. Every returned Sites deployment URL is a production URL. If the initial state is non-terminal or the user asks for progress, use get_deployment_status.

project_id.description: Exact opaque site project ID. Copy it verbatim from .openai/hosting.json's project_id or the id field returned by create_site, list_sites, or get_site. Never invent, modify, or substitute another identifier.
project_id.title: Project Id

version_id.description: Exact opaque saved version ID returned as id by save_site_version, list_site_versions, or get_site_version. Copy it verbatim as version_id; never substitute a project or deployment ID.
version_id.title: Version Id

id.description: Opaque deployment ID. Pass this exact value as deployment_id.
id.title: Id

project_id.description: Opaque site project ID. Pass this exact value as project_id.
project_id.title: Project Id

version_id.description: Opaque saved version ID. Pass this exact value as version_id.
version_id.title: Version Id

tool.name: sites.deploy_site_version
tool.title: deploy_site_version
tool.description: Deploy a saved site version to production when the site is shared with anyone besides the current caller, public, cannot be verified as owner-only, or when deploy_private_site_version is unavailable. This is an open-world deployment and requires explicit user approval. For a verified owner-only site, use deploy_private_site_version when available. An unsaved local build cannot be deployed directly. Every returned Sites deployment URL is a production URL. If the initial state is non-terminal or the user asks for progress, use get_deployment_status.

project_id.description: Exact opaque site project ID. Copy it verbatim from .openai/hosting.json's project_id or the id field returned by create_site, list_sites, or get_site. Never invent, modify, or substitute another identifier.
project_id.title: Project Id

version_id.description: Exact opaque saved version ID returned as id by save_site_version, list_site_versions, or get_site_version. Copy it verbatim as version_id; never substitute a project or deployment ID.
version_id.title: Version Id

id.description: Opaque deployment ID. Pass this exact value as deployment_id.
id.title: Id

project_id.description: Opaque site project ID. Pass this exact value as project_id.
project_id.title: Project Id

version_id.description: Opaque saved version ID. Pass this exact value as version_id.
version_id.title: Version Id

tool.name: sites.generate_siwc_bypass_token
tool.title: generate_siwc_bypass_token
tool.description: Generate a bearer token for identity-less API requests that bypasses a site's Sign in with ChatGPT gate. Use `get_site` to retrieve an existing token without rotating it. Calling this tool creates a token if none exists, or rotates and immediately invalidates the existing token. Pass the returned token as `OAI-Sites-Authorization: Bearer {siwc_bypass_bearer_token}`.

project_id.description: Exact opaque site project ID. Copy it verbatim from .openai/hosting.json's project_id or the id field returned by create_site, list_sites, or get_site. Never invent, modify, or substitute another identifier.
project_id.title: Project Id

siwc_bypass_bearer_token.description: Bearer token accepted by Sites dispatch in the OAI-Sites-Authorization header.
siwc_bypass_bearer_token.title: Siwc Bypass Bearer Token

tool.name: sites.get_deployment_status
tool.title: get_deployment_status
tool.description: Get the current status of a production deployment. Only poll when a deployment ID is available. Continue polling a non-terminal deployment when progress is requested, unless the user asks to stop. On success, report the production URL. On failure, report the failure message and the site, version, and deployment IDs.

project_id.description: ID of the site from the same deployment flow as version_id and deployment_id.
project_id.title: Project Id

version_id.description: Saved version ID from the same deployment flow as project_id and deployment_id.
version_id.title: Version Id

deployment_id.description: Deployment ID returned for the supplied project_id and version_id. Do not mix identifiers from different deployment flows.
deployment_id.title: Deployment Id

id.description: Opaque deployment ID. Pass this exact value as deployment_id.
id.title: Id

project_id.description: Opaque site project ID. Pass this exact value as project_id.
project_id.title: Project Id

version_id.description: Opaque saved version ID. Pass this exact value as version_id.
version_id.title: Version Id

_meta.connector_description: Use Sites to build, save, deploy, and inspect websites such as landing pages, portfolios, dashboards, portals, trackers, hubs, games, and internal tools. Always use Sites when .openai/hosting.json exists. Use Sites skills for local implementation, validation, source preparation, and artifact packaging. Use this connector for site creation, runtime environment variables, versions, production deployments, and access controls. Read .openai/hosting.json before creating a site and reuse its project_id when present. Treat Sites IDs and cursors as opaque: copy them exactly from .openai/hosting.json or Sites responses as applicable, and never invent, reformat, derive, or substitute them. Never call create_site more than once for the same local site. Push the exact source state before saving a version. commit_sha must identify that pushed state, and any archive must be built from it. Deploy only saved versions; every Sites deployment URL is production. Inspect deployment status when the initial result is non-terminal or the user asks for progress. Unless the user asks for local-only work or a saved version without deployment, finish deployable site work with a production deployment.

Unless the user asks for local-only work or a saved version without deployment, finish deployable site work with a production deployment.

tool.name: sites.save_site_version
tool.title: save_site_version
tool.description: Save a site version only after validating and pushing its source. commit_sha must be the current HEAD of the site's configured source branch. Any archive must come from that exact source state and contain a deployable Sites build. Saving does not deploy the version. Retain version_id for follow-up calls and report the user-facing version number.

project_id.description: Exact opaque site project ID. Copy it verbatim from .openai/hosting.json's project_id or the id field returned by create_site, list_sites, or get_site. Never invent, modify, or substitute another identifier.
project_id.title: Project Id

commit_sha.description: Git commit SHA for the current HEAD of the site's configured source branch. It must identify the source used to build the archive.
commit_sha.title: Commit Sha

anyOf.description:
Canonical shape for file args after proxying uploaded files.

This is sent to servers so they can fetch the file contents via the download URL
while also receiving the underlying file id. Optional mime_type and file_name
may be included when supported by the server schema.
anyOf.title: ProvidedFilePayload

archive.description: Optional site build tar archive from the source identified by commit_sha. It must contain a supported OpenNext or vinext entrypoint and a valid .openai/hosting.json.

id.description: Opaque saved version ID. Pass this exact value as version_id.
id.title: Id

project_id.description: Opaque site project ID. Pass this exact value as project_id.
project_id.title: Project Id

tool.title: update_environment_variables
tool.description: Update production runtime environment variables for a site. Only listed keys change; all others remain unchanged. Store runtime values in Sites, not .openai/hosting.json. Deploy a saved version after any change to apply the new environment revision.

project_id.description: Exact opaque site project ID. Copy it verbatim from .openai/hosting.json's project_id or the id field returned by create_site, list_sites, or get_site. Never invent, modify, or substitute another identifier.

set_values.description: Environment entries to create or replace. Keys are case-sensitive and must match the application. Do not repeat keys or include a key also listed in remove. Mark sensitive values as secrets.

key.description: Required non-empty, case-sensitive environment variable name.
key.title: Key

is_secret.description: Set true for sensitive values so they are not returned in plaintext.
is_secret.title: Is Secret

set_values.title: Set Values

remove.description: Case-sensitive environment keys to remove. Do not repeat keys or include a key also present in set_values. Omit or pass an empty list to preserve other keys.

allowed_tenant_group_ids.description: Tenant group ID allowlist. IDs must come from list_available_access_groups and belong to the tenant linked to the site workspace. Omit to preserve the existing allowlist; pass an empty list to clear it.

tools.namespace_description: Use Codex Document Control to find connected document sessions, inspect the tools supported by a selected session, and execute one supported tool against that session. Call `list_document_sessions` first to choose the intended connected session, call `get_document_tool_schemas` before constructing tool arguments, then call `execute_document_command` with a caller-stable `idempotency_key`. Use this only for connected Codex document control; do not use it for general spreadsheet, presentation, or document tasks without a connected document session.

tool.name: codex_document_control.execute_document_command
tool.title: execute_document_command
tool.description: Execute one supported surface-specific tool against a connected Codex document session. First call `list_document_sessions` to choose the intended `executor_session_id` and `supported_tools[].name`, then call `get_document_tool_schemas` for the selected `surface`, that `supported_tools[].name` as `tool_name`, and `version` before constructing `args`. `idempotency_key` must be a caller-stable key that you reuse verbatim only when retrying the same logical document-control command; use a new key for a different command.

executor_session_id.description: Exact `executor_session_id` copied from the selected Codex document session returned by `list_document_sessions`.
executor_session_id.title: Executor Session Id

idempotency_key.description: Caller-stable idempotency key for this logical document-control command. Reuse the exact same key only for retries of the same command.
idempotency_key.title: Idempotency Key

args.description: JSON object of arguments matching the selected tool's `input_schema` from `get_document_tool_schemas`.
args.title: Args

result.description: The server's response to a tool call.

items.description: Exact tool schema lookup keys from Codex document session discovery, keyed by `surface`, `supported_tools[].name` passed as `tool_name`, and `version`.

surface.description: Document surface. Use `excel` for Excel workbooks, `powerpoint` for PowerPoint presentations, or `sheets` for Google Sheets spreadsheets.
surface.title: Surface

tool_name.description: Exact `supported_tools[].name` copied from the selected session.
tool_name.title: Tool Name

version.description: Exact `supported_tools[].version` copied from the selected session.
version.title: Version

items.title: Items

tools.namespace_description: Plugin Management: uninstall: uninstall_app; install/connect: api_tool.search_plugins then api_tool.suggest_installs. Codex uninstall: plugin_management.uninstall_plugin; install: request_plugin_install. Use Plugin Management, not named plugin tools, for permissions/removal/deps. Clarify ambiguity with exact IDs. Route named-plugin access questions and approval-label requests (Always ask, Any changes, Important actions, Never ask, Use my default), even without permission wording. Call the matching tool for clear targets/modes or global/default changes. Ask without calling for missing/broad targets (Google), vague/conflicting modes, risky removal, or delegated choice. Disable is unsupported; explain without calling. Exclude how-to/undo, OAuth/admin scopes, npm/Chrome/code plugins, and ordinary use. Complete all actions; report only confirmed results.

tool.name: plugin_management.get_app_permissions
tool.title: get_app_permissions
tool.description: Inspect one named ChatGPT plugin's global/default and plugin-specific permission settings. Use when the user asks what the plugin may read, write, or do, whether it must ask first, or whether it inherits the default. For a missing/broad target such as my plugins, all, or Google, make no call and ask which plugin. Never pass global. Do not use for OAuth/admin scopes, install/connect/undo requests, ordinary plugin use, or npm/Chrome/code plugins.

app_id.description: ChatGPT plugin reference to inspect. May be a plugin id, connector id, platform slug, or unambiguous user-facing plugin name. It must identify one plugin; never pass all, global, Google, or another broad/generic target.
app_id.title: App Id

tool.name: plugin_management.get_plugin_dependencies
tool.title: get_plugin_dependencies
tool.description: Resolve the canonical public plugins declared by one plugin's app manifest. Use only when a skill or user explicitly asks for dependency metadata. Pass a plugin ID or name@marketplace reference unchanged. Named references resolve by globally listed plugin name. This reports metadata plus current user-aware plugin status, installation policy, and installed state; it does not install or connect anything. The result separates visible canonical plugins from app entries that lack a unique canonical plugin or whose canonical plugin is unavailable to the current user.

plugin_reference.description: Plugin ID or name@marketplace reference whose manifest dependencies should be resolved. Pass it unchanged.
plugin_reference.title: Plugin Reference

tool.name: plugin_management.uninstall_app
tool.title: uninstall_app
tool.description: Uninstall ChatGPT plugins only for explicit uninstall, remove, or disconnect intent. Pass every exact, user-approved target in one call. For a missing/broad target such as Google, all/risky plugins, or a choice left to you, make no call and ask. Disable is not uninstall. Never use this for install/connect/undo/how-to, sentiment, negation, ordinary plugin use, or npm/Chrome/code plugins. The result reports each outcome.

app_ids.description: Exact, user-approved ChatGPT plugin references to uninstall. Each item may be a plugin id, connector id, platform slug, or unambiguous user-facing name. Never pass Google or another broad provider, all/risky plugins, or a target chosen by the assistant.
app_ids.title: App Ids

reason.description: Optional user-visible reason for uninstalling the plugin.
reason.title: Reason

tool.name: plugin_management.update_app_permissions
tool.title: update_app_permissions
tool.description: Update global ChatGPT plugin permissions or a plugin-specific override. Omit app_id for global-only updates and provide it for plugin-specific updates. Map Always ask to always_ask, Any changes to ask_before_writes, Important actions to review_important_actions, Never ask to full_access, and Use my default to inherit. For plugin-specific changes, a missing/broad target such as Google, a vague mode such as tighter/more permissive, conflicting intent such as less access plus Never ask, or a choice left to you requires a question and no tool call; explicit global/default changes need no app_id. Never infer a mode or probe with get_app_permissions. One call may include both global_permissions and app_permissions with app_id; the global change is applied first. For several plugins call once per target and complete every requested update.

setting.description: Permission setting to update. This field is optional; omit it unless needed. If provided, use permission_mode.
setting.title: Setting

value.description: New value for the global permission setting. Options: always_ask (UI label: Always ask; ask before reading or making changes), ask_before_writes (UI label: Allow read actions; read without asking but ask before making changes), review_important_actions (UI label: Allow low-risk actions; automatically approve low-risk actions but may deny actions involving sensitive information), and full_access (UI label: Allow all actions; read or take action without asking; elevated risk and may be unavailable globally when the feature gate hides it).
value.title: Value

global_permissions.description: Global default permission updates to apply.
global_permissions.title: Global Permissions

setting.description: Permission setting to update. This field is optional; omit it unless needed. If provided, use permission_mode.
setting.title: Setting

value.description: New value for the plugin-specific permission setting. Options: inherit (UI label: Use default or follow global; clear this plugin's override), always_ask (UI label: Always ask; ask before reading or making changes with this plugin), ask_before_writes (UI label: Allow read actions; read without asking but ask before making changes with this plugin), review_important_actions (UI label: Allow low-risk actions; automatically approve low-risk actions with this plugin but may deny actions involving sensitive information), and full_access (UI label: Allow all actions; read or take action with this plugin without asking; elevated risk).
value.title: Value

updates.title: AppPermissionUpdateSet
updates.description: Permission updates to apply. A call may contain global_permissions, app_permissions, or both; app_permissions requires app_id.

app_id.description: Optional ChatGPT plugin identifier. Required for app_permissions updates; omit for global_permissions-only updates. May be a plugin id, connector id, platform slug, or unambiguous user-facing plugin name. Never pass Google or another broad/generic target.
app_id.title: App Id

reason.description: Optional user-visible reason for changing permissions.
reason.title: Reason

tools.namespace_description: Gmail tools for label counts, searching and reading emails/threads/attachments, reviewing drafts, and explicit mail changes like send, draft, forward, archive, Trash, and label actions.

tool.name: gmail.apply_labels_to_emails
tool.title: apply_labels_to_emails
tool.description: Apply labels to Gmail messages using label names rather than Gmail label IDs. This is the preferred labeling action for models because it avoids a separate label-id lookup step. Prefer this when the user refers to labels by name.

message_ids.description: Gmail message IDs returned by Gmail search/read results. Use `message_ids` from search_email_ids or `id` fields from email results. Do not pass placeholder values like `dummy`, `latest`, `gmail:<id>`, draft IDs, thread IDs, email addresses, subjects, or Gmail UI URLs.
message_ids.title: Message Ids

add_label_names.description: Gmail label display names. This action accepts names and can create missing labels when create_missing_labels is true; batch_modify_email requires existing Gmail label IDs.
add_label_names.title: Add Label Names

remove_label_names.description: Gmail label display names. This action accepts names and can create missing labels when create_missing_labels is true; batch_modify_email requires existing Gmail label IDs.
remove_label_names.title: Remove Label Names

create_missing_labels.description: Whether to create missing labels before applying them.
create_missing_labels.title: Create Missing Labels

success.description: Whether the label update request succeeded.
success.title: Success

added_label_ids.description: Label IDs added to the target messages.
added_label_ids.title: Added Label Ids

removed_label_ids.description: Label IDs removed from the target messages.
removed_label_ids.title: Removed Label Ids

created_labels.description: New labels created while applying the update.
created_labels.title: Created Labels

tool.name: gmail.archive_emails
tool.title: archive_emails
tool.description: Archive one or more existing Gmail messages by removing Gmail's INBOX label. Use this when the user wants messages removed from the inbox but kept in Gmail. The messages remain in Gmail and can still be found later.

message_ids.description: Gmail message IDs returned by Gmail search/read results. Use `message_ids` from search_email_ids or `id` fields from email results. Do not pass placeholder values like `dummy`, `latest`, `gmail:<id>`, draft IDs, thread IDs, email addresses, subjects, or Gmail UI URLs.
message_ids.title: Message Ids

message_id.description: Gmail message ID that the action targeted.
message_id.title: Message Id

success.description: Whether the email action succeeded.
success.title: Success

error.description: Short error code or summary, if the action failed.
error.title: Error

tool.name: gmail.batch_modify_email
tool.title: batch_modify_email
tool.description: Add or remove Gmail labels on a batch of individual messages. This modifies messages, not whole threads. To label by subject, sender, or search query, search first or use bulk_label_matching_emails/apply_labels_to_emails.

message_ids.description: Gmail message IDs returned by Gmail search/read results. Use `message_ids` from search_email_ids or `id` fields from email results. Do not pass placeholder values like `dummy`, `latest`, `gmail:<id>`, draft IDs, thread IDs, email addresses, subjects, or Gmail UI URLs.
message_ids.title: Message Ids

add_labels.description: Existing Gmail label IDs to add, not label display names. Prefer apply_labels_to_emails when you have label names or want missing labels created. Do not pass search operators such as -in:trash, ALL, or display names.
add_labels.title: Add Labels

remove_labels.description: Existing Gmail label IDs to remove, not label display names. Prefer apply_labels_to_emails when you have label names. Do not pass search operators such as -in:trash, ALL, or display names.
remove_labels.title: Remove Labels

success.description: Whether the batch modify request succeeded.
success.title: Success

tool.name: gmail.batch_read_email
tool.title: batch_read_email
tool.description: Read multiple Gmail messages in a single call. Each successful result includes the message body plus metadata such as sender/recipient fields, subject, snippet, labels, timestamp, and attachment metadata.

message_ids.description: Gmail message IDs returned by Gmail search/read results. Use `message_ids` from search_email_ids or `id` fields from email results. Do not pass placeholder values like `dummy`, `latest`, `gmail:<id>`, draft IDs, thread IDs, email addresses, subjects, or Gmail UI URLs.
message_ids.title: Message Ids

max_results.description: Ignored compatibility alias; message_ids controls the batch.
max_results.title: Max Results

max_messages.description: Ignored compatibility alias; message_ids controls the batch.
max_messages.title: Max Messages

max_output_tokens.description: Ignored compatibility alias; output size is not token-limited here.
max_output_tokens.title: Max Output Tokens

filename.description: Exact attachment file name. Pass this as read_attachment.filename when attachment_id is absent or marked truncated.
filename.title: Filename

size_bytes.description: Attachment size in bytes, if known.
size_bytes.title: Size Bytes

attachment_id.description: Provider Gmail body.attachmentId for this exact attachment. Pass this value as read_attachment.attachment_id only when non-null and complete; otherwise use filename.
attachment_id.title: Attachment Id

read_attachment_supported.description: Whether Gmail read_attachment supports this MIME type. Call read_attachment only when this is true. When false, do not call read_attachment; unsupported types fail with HTTP 415.
read_attachment_supported.title: Read Attachment Supported

responses.description: Batch email read results or per-message errors.
responses.title: Responses

error.description: Short error code or summary.
error.title: Error

filename.description: Exact inline image file name. Pass this as read_attachment.filename when attachment_id is absent or marked truncated.
filename.title: Filename

size_bytes.description: Inline image size in bytes, if known.
size_bytes.title: Size Bytes

attachment_id.description: Provider Gmail body.attachmentId for this exact inline image. Pass this value as read_attachment.attachment_id only when non-null and complete; otherwise use filename.
attachment_id.title: Attachment Id

content_id.description: Content-ID referenced by the email body; not valid for read_attachment.attachment_id.
content_id.title: Content Id

x_attachment_id.description: X-Attachment-Id referenced by the email body; not valid for read_attachment.attachment_id.
x_attachment_id.title: X Attachment Id

content_location.description: Content-Location referenced by the email body; not valid for read_attachment.attachment_id.
content_location.title: Content Location

id.description: Gmail message ID.
id.title: Id

body.description: Full email body content as text.
body.title: Body

attachments.description: Attachment summaries for this message. To read one, pass this message's id as message_id and either the entry's non-null attachment_id or its exact filename; do not invent attachment IDs.
attachments.title: Attachments

inline_images.description: Inline body images for this message. To read one, pass this message's id as message_id and either the entry's non-null attachment_id or its exact filename; do not use Content-ID or X-Attachment-Id as attachment_id.
inline_images.title: Inline Images

raw_mime.description: Original RFC822 MIME source for the message when read_email.include_raw_mime is true. Useful for inspecting raw HTML, MIME boundaries, and exact content headers.
raw_mime.title: Raw Mime

tool.name: gmail.read_attachment
tool.title: read_attachment
tool.description: Read one attachment from a Gmail message. First read/search the parent message and select an entry from its attachments or inline_images. For an attachments entry, call this action only when its read_attachment_supported field is true; when false, do not call this action because the MIME type is unsupported. Pass the parent message id as message_id. Prefer the entry's non-null attachment_id when its complete value is available; when it is absent or marked truncated, pass the exact filename instead. Do not synthesize attachment IDs from filenames, content IDs, x-attachment IDs, URLs, or user text.

attachment_id.description: Exact Gmail attachment_id copied from the selected attachment's attachments[].attachment_id or inline_images[].attachment_id on the parent message. Use it only when the complete value is available; if it is absent or marked truncated in a tool response, pass the exact filename instead. Do not pass truncated values, filenames, message IDs, thread IDs, Content-ID, X-Attachment-Id, URLs, or guessed values.

tool.name: gmail.search_email_ids
tool.title: search_email_ids
tool.description: Retrieve Gmail message IDs that match a search. If the user asks for important emails, search likely candidates and read/interpret them instead of treating Gmail system labels as the answer. Prefer list_labels for label counts. Put Gmail search operators in query, not label_ids.

query.description: Gmail search query. Put Gmail search operators here, including -in:spam, -in:trash, -category:promotions, category:promotions, label:<display name>, from:, to:, after:, before:, newer_than:, and has:attachment.
query.title: Query

label_ids.description: Optional Gmail label IDs, not Gmail search operators and not display names. Use exact label IDs such as INBOX, UNREAD, SENT, TRASH, SPAM, CATEGORY_PROMOTIONS, or user label IDs returned in list_labels.labels[].id. Put Gmail search syntax such as -in:spam, -in:trash, -category:promotions, label:Newsletters, category:promotions, newer_than:7d, or from:alice@example.com in query. Do not pass ALL, label display names like Newsletters, or custom names like DA/30 Waiting - Cody unless list_labels returned that exact value as id.
label_ids.title: Label Ids

max_results.description: Maximum number of results to return. Must be at least 1.
max_results.title: Max Results

next_page_token.description: Pagination token from a previous search.
next_page_token.title: Next Page Token

tool.name: gmail.search_emails
tool.title: search_emails
tool.description: Search Gmail for emails matching a query or exact label IDs. If the user asks for important emails, search likely candidates and read/interpret them instead of treating Gmail system labels as the answer. Prefer list_labels for count questions about inbox, unread, or other label totals. Put all Gmail search operators in query, including after:, before:, from:, to:, subject:, has:attachment, -in:spam, -in:trash, -category:promotions, and label:<display name>. Examples: query="-in:spam -in:trash", label_ids=None; query="", label_ids=["INBOX", "UNREAD"]; query="label:Newsletters newer_than:30d", label_ids=None. Non-examples: label_ids=["-in:spam"], label_ids=["ALL"], label_ids=["Newsletters"].

query.description: Gmail search query. Put Gmail search operators here, including -in:spam, -in:trash, -category:promotions, category:promotions, label:<display name>, from:, to:, after:, before:, newer_than:, and has:attachment.
query.title: Query

label_ids.description: Optional Gmail label IDs, not Gmail search operators and not display names. Use exact label IDs such as INBOX, UNREAD, SENT, TRASH, SPAM, CATEGORY_PROMOTIONS, or user label IDs returned in list_labels.labels[].id. Put Gmail search syntax such as -in:spam, -in:trash, -category:promotions, label:Newsletters, category:promotions, newer_than:7d, or from:alice@example.com in query. Do not pass ALL, label display names like Newsletters, or custom names like DA/30 Waiting - Cody unless list_labels returned that exact value as id.
label_ids.title: Label Ids

max_results.description: Maximum number of results to return. Must be at least 1.
max_results.title: Max Results

next_page_token.description: Pagination token from a previous search.
next_page_token.title: Next Page Token

filename.description: Exact attachment file name. Pass this as read_attachment.filename when attachment_id is absent or marked truncated.
filename.title: Filename

size_bytes.description: Attachment size in bytes, if known.
size_bytes.title: Size Bytes

attachment_id.description: Provider Gmail body.attachmentId for this exact attachment. Pass this value as read_attachment.attachment_id only when non-null and complete; otherwise use filename.
attachment_id.title: Attachment Id

read_attachment_supported.description: Whether Gmail read_attachment supports this MIME type. Call read_attachment only when this is true. When false, do not call read_attachment; unsupported types fail with HTTP 415.
read_attachment_supported.title: Read Attachment Supported

id.description: Gmail message ID.
id.title: Id

attachments.description: Attachment summaries for this message. To read one, pass this message's id as message_id and either the entry's non-null attachment_id or its exact filename; do not invent attachment IDs.
attachments.title: Attachments

inline_images.description: Inline body images for this message. To read one, pass this message's id as message_id and either the entry's non-null attachment_id or its exact filename; do not use Content-ID or X-Attachment-Id as attachment_id.
inline_images.title: Inline Images

filename.description: Exact inline image file name. Pass this as read_attachment.filename when attachment_id is absent or marked truncated.
filename.title: Filename

size_bytes.description: Inline image size in bytes, if known.
size_bytes.title: Size Bytes

attachment_id.description: Provider Gmail body.attachmentId for this exact inline image. Pass this value as read_attachment.attachment_id only when non-null and complete; otherwise use filename.
attachment_id.title: Attachment Id

content_id.description: Content-ID referenced by the email body; not valid for read_attachment.attachment_id.
content_id.title: Content Id

x_attachment_id.description: X-Attachment-Id referenced by the email body; not valid for read_attachment.attachment_id.
x_attachment_id.title: X Attachment Id

content_location.description: Content-Location referenced by the email body; not valid for read_attachment.attachment_id.
content_location.title: Content Location

next_page_token.description: Pagination token for the next result page, if available.
next_page_token.title: Next Page Token

tool.name: google_calendar.create_event

tool.description: Create a new Google Calendar event and return its details. Use this only when the user explicitly wants a calendar event, focus block, hold, or meeting created. If `add_google_meet` is true, Google may return a pending conference state before the Meet link is fully provisioned. Re-read the event later if you need finalized conference details.

reminders.description: Event reminder configuration. Use the calendar defaults when omitted.

guests_can_modify.description: Whether invited guests may modify the event. Set true only when the user explicitly wants guests to edit the event; leave null for Google default behavior.

tool.name: google_calendar.get_availability
tool.title: get_availability
tool.description: Look up busy windows on one or more calendars before scheduling a meeting. Use this action when the user wants availability for a coworker, room, or other known calendar ID. `time_min` and `time_max` must be full RFC3339 datetimes with `Z` or an explicit UTC offset. `response_timezone_str` controls only how Google formats the busy window timestamps in the response. This action returns busy windows only, not event titles or details, and inaccessible calendars are reported as per-calendar errors.

time_max.description: Required RFC3339 datetime string with `Z` or an explicit UTC offset (for example `2026-05-01T10:00:00-07:00`). Do not pass naive datetimes and do not pass `now`.

response_timezone_str.description: Required IANA timezone name used for response timestamps only, such as `America/Los_Angeles` or `Europe/Berlin`. This does not define the query interval.

tool.name: google_calendar.search
tool.title: search
tool.description: Search Google Calendar events within a time window. To obtain the full information for an event, use read_event. Accepted parameters are only `query`, `max_results`, `time_min`, and `time_max`. `query` is broad free text, not a structured search language. Prefer passing explicit `time_min` and `time_max` for every search, then page with `next_page_token` inside that bounded window before widening the query. Do not pass unsupported fields like `topn`, `timezone_str`, `calendar_id`, `user_message`, or `best_effort_fetch`.

hangout_link.description: Google Meet or Hangouts link for the event.

_meta.connector_description: Google Calendar tools for searching/reading events, checking availability before scheduling, reading colors, and explicit calendar changes: create/update/delete events or respond to invitations.

tools.namespace_description: Google Calendar tools for searching/reading events, checking availability before scheduling, reading colors, and explicit calendar changes: create/update/delete events or respond to invitations.

tool.name: google_calendar.search_events
tool.title: search_events
tool.description: Look up Google Calendar events using various filters. Use this to find candidate events before reading or changing a specific event. `query` is broad free text, not a structured search language. Prefer passing explicit `time_min` and `time_max` for every search, then page with `next_page_token` inside that bounded window before widening the query.

time_min.description: Start of the search window. Prefer passing an explicit full ISO-8601/RFC3339 datetime (for example `2026-05-01T00:00:00Z`) rather than omitting bounds. Use exact `now` only when you intentionally want a current boundary. Do not use relative expressions like `now-7d` or `now+30m`.
time_min.title: Time Min

time_max.description: End of the search window. Prefer passing an explicit full ISO-8601/RFC3339 datetime (for example `2026-05-31T23:59:59Z`) rather than omitting bounds. Use exact `now` only when you intentionally want a current boundary. Do not use relative expressions like `now-7d` or `now+30m`.
time_max.title: Time Max

timezone_str.description: Timezone for interpreting time_min/time_max. IANA timezone name such as `America/Los_Angeles` or `Europe/Berlin`. Do not pass UTC offsets like `+02:00`. Default is `America/Los_Angeles`.
timezone_str.title: Timezone Str

max_results.description: Maximum number of events to return. Must be at least 1.
max_results.title: Max Results

query.description: Broad free-text query passed to Google Calendar's `q` search parameter. Best for keyword matches in titles and some indexed event text, not precise attendee filtering.
query.title: Query

calendar_id.description: Calendar ID to query. Use `primary` for the user's main calendar, or an email-like calendar ID containing `@` (for example `team@group.calendar.google.com`). Default is `primary`.
calendar_id.title: Calendar Id

next_page_token.description: Pagination token returned by a previous search_events/search_events_all_fields call. Use it to continue paging within the same bounded window, and omit it on the first page.
next_page_token.title: Next Page Token

id.description: Google Calendar event ID.
id.title: Id

color_id.description: Google Calendar event color ID, if set.
color_id.title: Color Id

url.description: Browser URL for the calendar event.
url.title: Url

my_response_status.description: Authenticated user's response status for the event.
my_response_status.title: My Response Status

transparency.description: Busy/free transparency setting for the event.
transparency.title: Transparency

recurring_event_id.description: Recurring series ID when this event is part of a series.
recurring_event_id.title: Recurring Event Id

original_start_time.description: Original start time for recurring instances, if applicable.
original_start_time.title: Original Start Time

next_page_token.description: Pagination token for the next result page, if available.
next_page_token.title: Next Page Token

_meta.connector_description: Google Calendar tools for searching/reading events, checking availability before scheduling, reading colors, and explicit calendar changes: create/update/delete events or respond to invitations.

tools.namespace_description: Google Calendar tools for searching/reading events, checking availability before scheduling, reading colors, and explicit calendar changes: create/update/delete events or respond to invitations.

tool.name: google_calendar.update_event
tool.title: update_event
tool.description: Update an existing Google Calendar event. Read the event first when changing attendees, recurrence, or time-sensitive details on recurring meetings. If `add_google_meet` is true, Google may return a pending conference state before the Meet link is fully provisioned. Re-read the event later if you need finalized conference details.

title.description: Title of the event. Leave null to keep the same title.
title.title: Title

start_time.description: New start datetime in full ISO-8601/RFC3339 format. Leave null to keep the same start time.
start_time.title: Start Time

end_time.description: New end datetime in full ISO-8601/RFC3339 format. Leave null to keep the same end time.
end_time.title: End Time

timezone_str.description: IANA timezone name such as `America/Los_Angeles` or `Europe/Berlin`. Do not pass UTC offsets like `+02:00`. Provide this whenever changing start_time and/or end_time.
timezone_str.title: Timezone Str

description.description: Description of the event. Leave null to keep the same description.
description.title: Description

location.description: Location of the event. Leave null to keep the same location.
location.title: Location

color_id.description: Optional Google Calendar event color string ID from the `event` palette returned by `get_colors`. Pass the palette key, not a background or foreground hex value. Leave null to use or keep the calendar default color.
color_id.title: Color Id

use_default.description: Whether to use the calendar's default reminders for this event.
use_default.title: Use Default

minutes.description: Minutes before the event when the reminder triggers.
minutes.title: Minutes

overrides.description: Custom reminder overrides. Provide an empty list with use_default=false to disable reminders for the event.
overrides.title: Overrides

reminders.description: Reminder configuration. Omit to keep existing reminders.

attendees_to_add.description: List of attendee email addresses to add to the event. Each entry must be an email address or `me`. Omit or pass an empty list to leave additions unchanged.
attendees_to_add.title: Attendees To Add

attendees_to_remove.description: List of attendee email addresses to remove from the event. Each entry must be an email address or `me`. Omit or pass an empty list to leave removals unchanged.
attendees_to_remove.title: Attendees To Remove

guests_can_modify.description: Whether invited guests may modify the event. Set true only when the user explicitly wants guests to edit the event; leave null to keep the existing setting unchanged.
guests_can_modify.title: Guests Can Modify

event_type.description: Type for status events (`focusTime`/`outOfOffice`). Google Calendar does not allow changing an existing event's eventType.
event_type.title: Event Type

chat_status.description: Chat status for focus time events
chat_status.title: Chat Status

visibility.description: Optional event visibility (`default`, `public`, or `private`). Leave null to keep existing visibility unchanged.
visibility.title: Visibility

transparency.description: Optional event transparency. Use `opaque` to block the time as busy, or `transparent` to keep the event from blocking the calendar so overlapping bookings can still be scheduled. Leave null to keep existing transparency unchanged.
transparency.title: Transparency

add_google_meet.description: Whether to request a Google Meet link for the event. Defaults to false so updates leave existing conference details unchanged unless a Meet link is explicitly requested. If conference creation is still pending, re-read the event later to check final Meet details.
add_google_meet.title: Add Google Meet

calendar_id.description: Calendar ID to query. Use `primary` for the user's main calendar, or an email-like calendar ID containing `@` (for example `team@group.calendar.google.com`). Default is `primary`.
calendar_id.title: Calendar Id

update_scope.description: How to apply this update to a recurring event. Use `this_instance` to update only the selected occurrence, `entire_series` to update the recurring series master and apply the change to the whole recurring series, or `this_and_following` to split the recurring series at the selected occurrence and apply changes from there onward. For non-recurring events, `entire_series` behaves the same as `this_instance`.
update_scope.title: Update Scope

recurrence.description: New raw Google/RFC5545 recurrence lines. Only valid for `entire_series` or `this_and_following`. Omit to leave recurrence unchanged. For `this_and_following`, omitting recurrence reuses the current series rule from the split point onward, including COUNT-based weekly-style splits derived from the selected occurrence.
recurrence.title: Recurrence

response_status.description: Attendance response status for the attendee.
response_status.title: Response Status

resource.description: Whether the attendee is a resource.
resource.title: Resource

is_self.description: Whether the attendee is the authenticated user.
is_self.title: Is Self

minutes.description: Minutes before the event when the reminder triggers.
minutes.title: Minutes

use_default.description: Whether to use the calendar's default reminders for this event.
use_default.title: Use Default

overrides.description: Custom reminder overrides. Provide an empty list with use_default=false to disable reminders for the event.
overrides.title: Overrides

id.description: Google Calendar event ID.
id.title: Id

color_id.description: Google Calendar event color ID, if set.
color_id.title: Color Id

transparency.description: Busy/free transparency setting for the event.
transparency.title: Transparency

hangout_link.description: Google Meet or Hangouts link for the event.
hangout_link.title: Hangout Link

conference_id.description: Conference identifier, if one was created.
conference_id.title: Conference Id

tools.namespace_description: Search and work with files from Google Drive, Docs, Sheets, and Slides.

tool.name: google_drive.batch_update_document
tool.title: batch_update_document
tool.description: Apply raw Google Docs batchUpdate requests to document content, not Drive file metadata.

requests.description: Raw Google Docs API documents.batchUpdate request objects for editing document content. Each list item must set exactly one request type key such as insertText, updateTextStyle, replaceAllText, deleteContentRange, insertInlineImage, or addDocumentTab. For insertInlineImage, pass a short public HTTP(S) URL string directly in uri. For local/generated image bytes, put the workspace image path in image_uris and set the matching request uri to a non-public placeholder such as that same path. Do not pass base64 data URLs directly. Send each request as a structured object in the list, not as a JSON string or other stringified input. Requests execute in order. Do not use this to rename or move the Drive file; use update_file for Drive metadata or parent-folder changes.
requests.title: Requests

document_url.description: Google Docs URL in the format https://docs.google.com/document/d/<DOCUMENT_ID>/... or a raw Google Docs document ID. If you only know the document title or title keywords, call `search_documents` first instead of asking the user for a URL. Do not pass document titles, Drive open?id links, app:// URLs, or /document/create.
document_url.title: Document Url

document_id.description: Raw Google Docs document ID only (for example `1abcDEF...`). Use this when you already have the ID from a prior search result. Do not pass a full URL here.
document_id.title: Document Id

requiredRevisionId.description: Require the document to still be at this revision ID or fail the batch update.
requiredRevisionId.title: Requiredrevisionid

targetRevisionId.description: Apply the batch update against this revision ID and merge with newer changes when possible.
targetRevisionId.title: Targetrevisionid

write_control.description: Optional writeControl object for the underlying Google Docs API batch update call.

anyOf.description: Connector-local file reference payload used for upload/download handoff.
anyOf.title: ConnectorFileReference

image_uris.description: Optional sidecar file references for local or generated images used by Drive roll-up batch update actions. This exists because runtime file upload rewriting currently only handles top-level file parameters. Put local workspace image paths here in the same order as the matching image URL placeholders in requests. Public HTTP(S) image URLs should stay directly in requests and should not be repeated here. Do not pass base64 data URLs.
image_uris.title: Image Uris

revisionId.description: Updated document revision ID, if available.
revisionId.title: Revisionid

replies.description: Replies returned by the batch update requests.
replies.title: Replies

writeControl.description: Write control state returned after the batch update, if available.

requiredRevisionId.description: Require the document to still be at this revision ID or fail the batch update.
requiredRevisionId.title: Requiredrevisionid

targetRevisionId.description: Apply the batch update against this revision ID and merge with newer changes when possible.
targetRevisionId.title: Targetrevisionid

tool.description: Apply raw Google Slides batchUpdate requests to presentation content, not Drive file metadata.

requests.description: Raw Google Slides API presentations.batchUpdate request objects for editing presentation content. Each list item must set exactly one request type key such as createSlide, createImage, insertText, updateTextStyle, replaceAllText, updatePageElementTransform, deleteObject, or duplicateObject. Use slide/page objectId values returned by get_presentation, get_presentation_outline, or get_slide for fields such as elementProperties.pageObjectId or slideObjectIds; do not use the presentation ID, slide number, layout ID, or a page element ID. For local/generated image bytes in createImage.url, replaceImage.url, or replaceAllShapesWithImage.imageUrl, put the workspace image path in image_uris and set the matching request URL field to a non-public placeholder such as that same path. Send each request as a structured object in the list, not as a JSON string or other stringified input. Requests execute in order. Do not use this to rename or move the Drive file; use update_file for Drive metadata or parent-folder changes.
requests.title: Requests

presentation_url.description: Google Slides URL in the format https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/<PRESENTATION_ID>/... or a raw presentation ID. If you only know the deck title or title keywords, call `search_presentations` first instead of asking the user for a URL.
presentation_url.title: Presentation Url

presentation_id.description: Raw Google Slides presentation ID only (for example `1abcDEF...`). Use this when you already have the ID from a prior search result. Do not pass a full URL here.
presentation_id.title: Presentation Id

requiredRevisionId.description: Require the presentation to still be at this revision ID or fail the batch update.
requiredRevisionId.title: Requiredrevisionid

write_control.description: Optional writeControl object for the underlying Google Slides API batch update call. Prefer providing requiredRevisionId from a fresh read before writing when you want concurrent edits to fail cleanly.

anyOf.description: Connector-local file reference payload used for upload/download handoff.
anyOf.title: ConnectorFileReference

image_uris.description: Optional sidecar file references for local or generated images used by Drive roll-up batch update actions. This exists because runtime file upload rewriting currently only handles top-level file parameters. Put local workspace image paths here in the same order as the matching image URL placeholders in requests. Public HTTP(S) image URLs should stay directly in requests and should not be repeated here. Do not pass base64 data URLs.
image_uris.title: Image Uris

writeControl.description: Write control state returned after the batch update, if available.

replies.description: Replies returned by the batch update requests.
replies.title: Replies

requiredRevisionId.description: Require the presentation to still be at this revision ID or fail the batch update.
requiredRevisionId.title: Requiredrevisionid

tool.name: google_drive.batch_update_spreadsheet
tool.title: batch_update_spreadsheet
tool.description: Apply raw Google Sheets batchUpdate requests to spreadsheet content, not Drive file metadata.

requests.description: Raw Google Sheets API batchUpdate requests, in execution order. Each item must be one structured Sheets REST request object with exactly one request type key, for example {'addSheet': {...}}, {'updateCells': {...}}, or {'findReplace': {...}}. Use Google field names and casing exactly and do not pass JSON strings. For updateCells, provide a valid start or range with the target sheetId, keep row/column indexes inside the requested grid, put the field mask on updateCells.fields, and do not put a fields key inside rows[]. For findReplace, set exactly one scope: range, sheetId, or allSheets. For local/generated image bytes in IMAGE formulas, put the workspace image path in image_uris and set the matching formula URL argument to a non-public placeholder such as that same path. Do not use this to rename or move the Drive file; use update_file for Drive metadata or parent-folder changes.
requests.title: Requests

spreadsheet_url.description: Google Sheets spreadsheet URL in the format https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/<SPREADSHEET_ID>/... or a raw spreadsheet ID. If you only know the spreadsheet title or title keywords, call `search_spreadsheets` first instead of asking the user for a URL.
spreadsheet_url.title: Spreadsheet Url

spreadsheet_id.description: Raw Google Sheets spreadsheet ID only (for example `1abcDEF...`). Use this when you already have the ID from a prior search result. Do not pass a full URL here.
spreadsheet_id.title: Spreadsheet Id

include_spreadsheet_in_response.description: When true, include the updated spreadsheet resource in the response.
include_spreadsheet_in_response.title: Include Spreadsheet In Response

response_ranges.description: Optional ranges to include in updatedSpreadsheet when include_spreadsheet_in_response is true. A1 range including the sheet name, e.g. Sheet1!A1:C20 or 'Q1 Plan'!A1:C20. Quote sheet names that contain spaces or punctuation and avoid duplicated sheet prefixes.
response_ranges.title: Response Ranges

response_include_grid_data.description: When true, include grid data in updatedSpreadsheet. Only meaningful when include_spreadsheet_in_response is true.
response_include_grid_data.title: Response Include Grid Data

anyOf.description: Connector-local file reference payload used for upload/download handoff.
anyOf.title: ConnectorFileReference

image_uris.description: Optional sidecar file references for local or generated images used by Drive roll-up batch update actions. This exists because runtime file upload rewriting currently only handles top-level file parameters. Put local workspace image paths here in the same order as the matching image URL placeholders in requests. Public HTTP(S) image URLs should stay directly in requests and should not be repeated here. Do not pass base64 data URLs.
image_uris.title: Image Uris

replies.description: Replies returned by the batch update requests.
replies.title: Replies

tool.name: google_drive.bulk_update_file_comments
tool.title: bulk_update_file_comments
tool.description: Create, reply to, and resolve Drive file comments in one bulk tool call. Before calling, inspect the file and decide on all intended comment updates for this file. Put top-level comments in `comments`, thread replies in `replies`, and resolved threads in `resolutions`. For each top-level comment, you must include enough location context for a reader to identify the exact target even if Google displays the Drive API comment as unanchored: use `quoted_text` with the exact sentence or phrase for Docs/text, use `slide_number` plus `quoted_text` when possible for Slides, and use `sheet_cell_range` with the sheet name and A1 cell/range for Sheets. Supports 1-20 total operations.

url.description: Google Drive/Docs/Sheets/Slides file URL containing a valid ID (for example https://drive.google.com/file/d/<FILE_ID>/... or https://docs.google.com/document/d/<FILE_ID>/...). Do not pass local filesystem paths, Windows paths, gdrive:// URIs, or plain names.
url.title: Url

id.description: Google Drive file ID only (for example `1abcDEF...`). Do not pass extra parameters.
id.title: Id

content.description: Plain-text content for the comment or reply.
content.title: Content

quoted_text.description: Optional exact text snippet from the file that this comment refers to. For Google Workspace editor files, prefer including this short snippet because Drive API-created anchors can appear unanchored in the editor UI.
quoted_text.title: Quoted Text

slide_number.description: Optional 1-based slide number this comment refers to for Google Slides files.
slide_number.title: Slide Number

sheet_cell_range.description: Optional Google Sheets A1 cell or range reference this comment refers to, such as `B12` or `Sheet1!B12:D15`.
sheet_cell_range.title: Sheet Cell Range

anchor.description: Optional raw Google Drive comment anchor JSON string. Omit to create an unanchored comment. Use this only when you already have a provider-valid anchor string; the connector does not construct anchors for you.
anchor.title: Anchor

comments.description: Top-level Drive file comments to create. Inspect the file first and collect all intended comments for this file before calling this action instead of calling once per comment. For every comment, you must include enough location context for a reader to identify the target even if Google shows the Drive API comment as unanchored: use `quoted_text` with the exact sentence or phrase for Docs/text, use `slide_number` plus `quoted_text` when possible for Slides, and use `sheet_cell_range` with the sheet name and A1 cell/range for Sheets.
comments.title: Comments

comment_id.description: Drive comment thread ID on the file.
comment_id.title: Comment Id

content.description: Plain-text content for the comment or reply.
content.title: Content

replies.description: Replies to add to existing Drive comment threads. Include all intended replies for this file in one call.
replies.title: Replies

comment_id.description: Drive comment thread ID on the file.
comment_id.title: Comment Id

reply_content.description: Optional reply text to include while resolving the comment. Omit to resolve without adding a note.
reply_content.title: Reply Content

resolutions.description: Existing Drive comment threads to resolve. Include all intended resolutions for this file in one call.
resolutions.title: Resolutions

id.description: Comment thread ID.
id.title: Id

htmlContent.description: Comment content as HTML, if available.
htmlContent.title: Htmlcontent

resolved.description: Whether the comment thread is resolved.
resolved.title: Resolved

anchor.description: Raw Drive anchor payload, if available.
anchor.title: Anchor

quotedFileContent.description: Quoted file content referenced by the comment.

me.description: Whether the author is the authenticated user.
me.title: Me

mimeType.description: MIME type for the quoted file content.
mimeType.title: Mimetype

value.description: Quoted file text associated with the comment.
value.title: Value

id.description: Reply ID.
id.title: Id

htmlContent.description: Reply content as HTML, if available.
htmlContent.title: Htmlcontent

action.description: Reply action such as resolve, if available.
action.title: Action

created_comments.description: Comments created by the bulk update.
created_comments.title: Created Comments

created_replies.description: Replies created by the bulk update.
created_replies.title: Created Replies

resolved_comments.description: Resolution replies created while resolving comments.
resolved_comments.title: Resolved Comments

total_operations.description: Total number of comment operations performed.
total_operations.title: Total Operations

tool.name: google_drive.get_profile
tool.title: get_profile
tool.description: Return the current Google Drive user's profile information. This action takes no parameters.

Profile.description:
Many connectors (especially OAuth-based) act on behalf of a user.
This class represents the user's profile, which is typically retrieved from
the connector's API during the OAuth flow.

Implementation details for different connectors vary, so we can't guarantee
that all fields will be present.
Profile.title: Profile

tool.description: Upload a local DOC/DOCX/ODT/RTF/HTML/TXT file to Drive, defaulting to native Google Docs.

Google's source of truth is the Drive API `about.importFormats` field: https://developers.google.com/workspace/drive/api/reference/rest/v3/about.

source_file.title: GoogleDocsImportSourceFile

title.description: Optional title for the imported Google Docs document. Defaults to the uploaded filename stem.

upload_mode.title: GoogleDocsImportUploadMode
upload_mode.description: How to store the uploaded file in Drive. Defaults to native_google_docs. `keep_source_file_type` preserves the uploaded file type, but the source file must still be one of the accepted Drive import MIME types for this action.

success.description: Whether the document import upload succeeded.

converted.description: Whether the uploaded document was converted into native Google Docs.

url.description: Browser URL for the imported Drive file, if available.

documentId.description: Google Docs document ID when converted to native Google Docs.

parentId.description: Destination Drive folder ID, if the file was uploaded there.

tools.namespace_description: Search and work with files from Google Drive, Docs, Sheets, and Slides.

tool.name: google_drive.search
tool.title: search
tool.description: Search Google Drive files by query and return basic details. Accepted parameters are only `query`, `topn`, `special_filter_query_str`, `best_effort_fetch`, `fetch_ttl`, and `require_viewed_by_user`. Use clear, specific keywords such as project names, collaborators, or file types. Example: ``"design doc pptx"``. When using query, each search query is an AND token match. Meaning, every token in the query is required to be present in order to match. - Search will return documents that contain all of the keywords in the query. - Therefore, queries should be short and keyword-focused (avoid long natural language). - If no results are found, try the following strategies: 1) Use different or related keywords. 2) Make the query more generic and simpler. - To improve recall, consider variants of your terms: abbreviations, synonyms, etc. - Previous search results can provide hints about useful variants of internal terms — use those to refine queries. Use `special_filter_query_str` when you need precise MIME-type or metadata filters. It uses Google Drive v3 search (the `q` parameter). - Supported time fields: `modifiedTime`, `createdTime`, `viewedByMeTime`, `sharedWithMeTime` (ISO 8601, e.g., '2025-09-03T00:00:00'). - People/ownership filters: `'me' in owners`, `'user@domain.com' in owners`, `'user@domain.com' in writers`, `'user@domain.com' in readers`, `sharedWithMe = true`. - Type filters: `mimeType = 'application/vnd.google-apps.document'` (Docs), `...spreadsheet` (Sheets), `...presentation` (Slides), and `mimeType != 'application/vnd.google-apps.folder'` to exclude folders. or mimeType = 'application/vnd.google-apps.folder' to select folders. Set `require_viewed_by_user=True` to restrict results to files the current user has viewed. Do not pass unsupported fields like `top_k`, `max_results`, `page_size`, `folder_url`, `query_type`, `user_message`, `recency_days`, `driveId`, or `include_shared_drives`.

query.description: Keyword query for Drive search. Use concise terms like project/file names. This may be empty only when `special_filter_query_str` is provided.
query.title: Query

topn.description: Maximum results to return. Parameter name is `topn` (not `top_k`, `max_results`, or `page_size`).
topn.title: Topn

special_filter_query_str.description: Optional raw Google Drive API `q` filter expression for advanced filtering.
special_filter_query_str.title: Special Filter Query Str

best_effort_fetch.description: When true, attempt to fetch text content for each result.
best_effort_fetch.title: Best Effort Fetch

fetch_ttl.description: Best-effort fetch timeout in seconds when best_effort_fetch=true.
fetch_ttl.title: Fetch Ttl

require_viewed_by_user.description: When true, keep only files viewed by the authenticated user.
require_viewed_by_user.title: Require Viewed By User

results.description: Matching Google Drive files and folders.
results.title: Results

query.description: String to search for in any cell within each row.
query.title: Query

start_row.description: 1-based first row to scan. Usually 1 when the header is in the first row.
start_row.title: Start Row

end_row.description: 1-based last row to scan. Required unless range is provided. Choose a finite bound from spreadsheet metadata or user context; this is the scan limit, not the result limit. The scan may cover at most 50,000 cells.
end_row.title: End Row

start_column.description: First spreadsheet column letter to scan, e.g. A. Usually A when scanning the visible table.
start_column.title: Start Column

end_column.description: Last spreadsheet column letter to scan, e.g. Z. Required unless range is provided. Choose a finite bound from spreadsheet metadata or known table width. The scan may cover at most 50,000 cells.
end_column.title: End Column

range.description: bounded A1 scan range, optional sheet, e.g. A1:F100 or Sheet1!A1:F100. The scan may cover at most 50,000 cells.
range.title: Range

spreadsheet_url.description: Google Sheets spreadsheet URL in the format https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/<SPREADSHEET_ID>/... or a raw spreadsheet ID. If you only know the spreadsheet title or title keywords, call `search_spreadsheets` first instead of asking the user for a URL.
spreadsheet_url.title: Spreadsheet Url

spreadsheet_id.description: Raw Google Sheets spreadsheet ID only (for example `1abcDEF...`). Use this when you already have the ID from a prior search result. Do not pass a full URL here.
spreadsheet_id.title: Spreadsheet Id

return_columns.description: Optional spreadsheet column letters to include in output, e.g. ['A', 'C', 'F']. They must fall inside the scanned column bounds. Leave null to return the first max_columns scanned columns.
return_columns.title: Return Columns

column_numbers.description: Deprecated compatibility alias for return_columns. 1-based column positions relative to the scanned range. Use null unless maintaining an older caller.
column_numbers.title: Column Numbers

header_row.description: 1-based spreadsheet row containing column headers. The default behaves like the previous search_spreadsheet_rows action: row 1 when included, otherwise the first scanned row. Use null when the scanned range has no header row.
header_row.title: Header Row

include_header_row.description: When true and header_row is inside the scan, include the header values as the first output row.
include_header_row.title: Include Header Row

max_matching_rows.description: Maximum number of matching non-header rows to return. This limits output only, not the scan. Default is 100.
max_matching_rows.title: Max Matching Rows

max_rows.description: Deprecated compatibility alias for max_matching_rows. Leave null for new calls.
max_rows.title: Max Rows

max_columns.description: Maximum number of scanned columns to return when return_columns is null. Default is 100.
max_columns.title: Max Columns

scanned_range.description: Bounded A1 range scanned by the action.
scanned_range.title: Scanned Range

scanned_row_count.description: Number of spreadsheet rows in the requested scan range.
scanned_row_count.title: Scanned Row Count

scanned_column_count.description: Number of spreadsheet columns in the requested scan range.
scanned_column_count.title: Scanned Column Count

matched_row_count.description: Number of non-header rows that matched the query.
matched_row_count.title: Matched Row Count

returned_matching_row_count.description: Number of matching non-header rows included in the markdown.
returned_matching_row_count.title: Returned Matching Row Count

truncated.description: Whether the returned markdown was truncated.
truncated.title: Truncated

truncated_rows.description: Whether rows were truncated from the output.
truncated_rows.title: Truncated Rows

truncated_columns.description: Whether columns were truncated from the output.
truncated_columns.title: Truncated Columns

truncated_row_count.description: Number of rows omitted from the output.
truncated_row_count.title: Truncated Row Count

truncated_column_count.description: Number of columns omitted from the output.
truncated_column_count.title: Truncated Column Count

tool.name: google_drive.share_file
tool.title: share_file
tool.description: Share a Drive file with a user or anyone at the company.

url.description: Google Drive file URL to share. Folder URLs are not accepted for this action.
url.title: Url

permission.title: SharePermission
permission.description: Share permission level to grant. Use `reader` for read-only access, `writer` to allow edits, `commenter` for comment-only access, or `owner` only when the API path supports ownership transfer.

user_email.description: Specific user email to share with. Provide this or set anyone_at_company=true.
user_email.title: User Email

anyone_at_company.description: Share with anyone in the Google Workspace domain.
anyone_at_company.title: Anyone At Company

show_in_search.description: When sharing with anyone_at_company, whether the file is discoverable in search.
show_in_search.title: Show In Search

success.description: Whether the share operation succeeded.
success.title: Success

_meta.connector_description: Search and work with files from Google Drive, Docs, Sheets, and Slides.

tools.namespace_description: Search and work with files from Google Drive, Docs, Sheets, and Slides.

tool.name: google_drive.update_file
tool.title: update_file
tool.description: Update an existing Drive file. Without `file_uri`, this updates metadata and parents only, including rename and move operations. With `file_uri`, this replaces the raw file bytes in place using Drive files.update upload semantics while preserving the same Drive file ID. Do not use Google Workspace MIME types with `file_uri`; native Docs/Sheets/Slides edits use their dedicated batch-update actions.

fileId.description: Google Drive API `fileId` path parameter. Raw file IDs are preferred; Drive/Docs/Sheets/Slides URLs are also accepted.
fileId.title: Fileid

name.description: Optional Google Drive file name. Use this to rename an existing Drive file. Leave null when only changing parents.
name.title: Name

addParents.description: Optional Google Drive API `addParents` query parameter: comma-separated parent folder IDs to add. For moving a file, set this to the destination folder ID.
addParents.title: Addparents

removeParents.description: Optional Google Drive API `removeParents` query parameter: comma-separated parent folder IDs to remove. For moving a file, set this to the current/source parent folder ID.
removeParents.title: Removeparents

anyOf.description: Connector-local file reference payload used for upload/download handoff.
anyOf.title: ConnectorFileReference

file_uri.description: Optional connector file reference whose bytes should replace the existing raw Drive file content. Leave null for a metadata-only rename or move. Do not pass raw local file paths or string URLs.
file_uri.title: File Uri

mime_type.description: Optional MIME type for the replacement bytes. Leave null to use the MIME type from file_uri, or application/octet-stream if file_uri does not include one. Do not use Google Workspace MIME types such as application/vnd.google-apps.document.
mime_type.title: Mime Type

success.description: Whether the update operation succeeded.
success.title: Success

accepted_input.description: Set to `file_uri` when the update consumed replacement bytes.
accepted_input.title: Accepted Input

id.description: Updated Google Drive file ID, if available.
id.title: Id

mime_type.description: Updated file MIME type, if available.
mime_type.title: Mime Type

url.description: Browser URL for the updated file, if available.
url.title: Url

parent_ids.description: Parent folder IDs after the update, if available.
parent_ids.title: Parent Ids

modified_time.description: Most recent modification timestamp after the update.
modified_time.title: Modified Time

size.description: Updated file size in bytes as returned by Drive, if available.
size.title: Size

tool.name: google_drive.upload_file
tool.title: upload_file
tool.description: Upload a file reference as a new Google Drive file, not rename or move an existing file.

anyOf.description: Connector-local file reference payload used for upload/download handoff.
anyOf.title: ConnectorFileReference

file_uri.description: Required file reference for the bytes to upload. Pass the `file_uri` object returned by another connector or generated by the runtime; if you provide it manually, send the same structured object/dictionary shape rather than base64 content. Do not pass raw local file paths or string URLs.
file_uri.title: File Uri

parent_folder_id.description: Optional destination Google Drive folder ID. Leave null to upload to My Drive/root. Do not pass folder URLs, folder names, `root`, or local filesystem paths.
parent_folder_id.title: Parent Folder Id

file_name.description: Optional destination file name including extension. Leave null to use the name from file_uri.
file_name.title: File Name

mime_type.description: Optional MIME type for the Drive file. Leave null to use the MIME type from file_uri.
mime_type.title: Mime Type

UploadResponse.description: Result from uploading a connector file reference as a normal Google Drive file.

accepted_input.description: The upload action consumed a connector file reference, not base64.
accepted_input.title: Accepted Input

success.description: Whether Google Drive accepted the upload.
success.title: Success

id.description: Google Drive file ID for the uploaded file.
id.title: Id

title.description: Uploaded file title in Google Drive.
title.title: Title

file_name.description: Uploaded file name in Google Drive.
file_name.title: File Name

mime_type.description: MIME type stored for the uploaded file.
mime_type.title: Mime Type

url.description: Browser URL for the uploaded Drive file.
url.title: Url

parent_id.description: Destination Drive folder ID, if set.
parent_id.title: Parent Id

UploadResponse.title: UploadResponse
